title: Floral Fiordilatte Delight
description: A creamy Chinese-inspired dish featuring fiordilatte, double-rise bread, and edible flowers. Perfect for a solo dinner
instructions: Slice the double-rise bread into 1-inch cubes.
In a bowl, whisk together honey, soy sauce, and grated ginger.
Dip each bread cube into the honey mixture, then coat with toasted sesame seeds.
Place the coated bread cubes on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 12 minutes, or until golden brown.
Meanwhile, slice the fiordilatte cheese into 1/2-inch thick slices.
In a small saucepan, melt the cheese over low heat, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped fresh mint leaves and chives to the melted cheese and stir to combine.
To assemble the dish, place a toasted bread cube on each plate, followed by a slice of the cheese mixture, and finish with a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds and thinly sliced scallions.
Garnish with additional fresh mint leaves, if desired.
